A Century of Mastery

We Do Not
Craft Jewelry.
We Architect Light.

Luvior was born from a singular obsession: the pursuit of the sublime. Our journey began in the private ateliers where we spent decades perfecting the intricate secrets of the world's most renowned luxury houses.

Today, Luvior stands as the definitive destination where the soul of an artist meets the hand of a master — offering the vision, the hand-rendered artistry, and the meticulous craftsmanship that only a century of experience can bestow.

The Art of Making

Techniques That Take a Lifetime to Master

Plique-à-jour
Translucent enamel cells with no metal backing — like stained glass, suspended in light. Each cell is hand-fired multiple times.
Bas-taille
Translucent enamel over engraved metal, creating depth that shifts with the light — every angle reveals a new dimension.
Champlevé
Enamel set flush within carved recesses in the gold — seamless, botanical, and indistinguishable from nature itself.
Martellato
The ancient Venetian art of hand-hammering gold to create organic, architectural textures that echo centuries of Italian craftsmanship.

© 2025 Luvior. All rights reserved. BIS Hallmarked · IGI Certified · 18K Gold

The Art of the Impossible

I

Where Every Piece Begins

At Luvior, every creation begins as a whisper of an idea, rendered manually by the designer's hand. Not a CAD file. Not a digital render. A sketch — drawn with the same pencil, in the same language, that has described extraordinary jewelry for over a century.

We believe that true luxury is the distance between the hand and the machine. Every curve in a Luvior piece was once a thought in a designer's mind, translated through fingertips onto paper before it was ever translated into gold.

Plique-à-jour
France · 16th Century
Transparent enamel in open metal cells — like a miniature stained-glass window, holding light within the jewelry itself. Used in the Luminous Vine bracelet.
Bas-taille
Italy · 14th Century
Translucent enamel applied over engraved gold, creating dimensional color that shifts as light moves. The deeper the engraving, the richer the hue.
Champlevé
Limoges · 12th Century
Enamel inlaid flush within recesses carved directly into the gold — seamless, botanical, as if the color grew from within the metal itself.
II

The Stone That Changes Everything

Luvior chose amethyst as its hero stone with deliberation. In the ancient world, amethyst was considered more precious than diamonds — worn by royalty, set in papal rings, and believed to possess the clarity of a cloudless Mediterranean sky at dusk.

Its deep purple is not the purple of fashion. It is the purple of the last pressing of winter grapes. The purple of Venice at midnight. The purple that appears, for one moment, in the sky above the Italian coast just before the stars arrive.

We source only stones that carry that particular quality of light. If an amethyst does not hold the sunset, it does not enter a Luvior piece.

1925
The first private atelier opens. Three craftsmen, one obsession: learning the secrets of Haute Joaillerie that the great European houses guard so carefully.
1962
Mastery of Plique-à-jour enamel — a technique that takes a decade to learn and that fewer than twenty craftsmen in the world execute at the highest level.
1988
The first bespoke commission for a private client — a rebirth of a 19th-century heirloom brooch. The beginning of a different kind of luxury: deeply, irreversibly personal.
2025
The Riviera Radiance collection. Five pieces. A hundred years of mastery. The first time Luvior has spoken to the world directly.
III

The Bespoke Promise

We do not offer bespoke jewelry. We offer bespoke stories. The process begins with a conversation — not about budget or stone size, but about the moment the piece is for. The chapter of your life it will mark. The woman who will wear it in forty years.

From that conversation comes a hand-sketch. From that sketch, a conversation about materials. From those materials, a masterpiece that carries, within its gold, a century of the knowledge of how to make something that will outlast everything else you own.

Begin a Commission

The House of Luvior

We Do Not Merely Craft Jewelry.
We Architect Light.

A heritage spanning one hundred years at the pinnacle of high-jewelry design and creation. Born from a singular obsession: the pursuit of the sublime.

Our Beginning

The Private Atelier

Luvior was born in a private atelier where we spent decades perfecting the intricate secrets of the world's most renowned luxury houses. Not copying them. Not being inspired by them. Learning, with complete commitment, the technical foundations upon which all extraordinary jewelry is built.

When you learn how Cartier thinks about a stone before it is set, how Van Cleef considers the weight of a pendant before the chain is chosen, you acquire a different eye. You begin to see jewelry not as a product, but as a consequence of decisions made at the level of light itself.

That is the Luvior difference. Not just the craft. The way of seeing that precedes it.

What We Believe

The Principles of the House

01
Heritage Over Trend
We do not follow seasons. We do not respond to trends. Our work is calibrated to outlast them. Every decision is made in the context of how the piece will look in a hundred years.
02
Hand Over Machine
The distance between the hand and the machine is where luxury lives. Every Luvior piece passes through human hands at every stage of its creation — from first sketch to final polish.
03
Story Over Object
We do not sell jewelry. We create the object around which stories collect. The piece you buy today will be the centerpiece of family conversations that have not yet begun.
